consequence: ADI must classify the exposure at the 30-day past-due bucket for prudential reporting purposes.
ADI must classify the exposure at the 60-day past-due bucket for prudential reporting purposes.
ADI must classify the exposure at the 90-day past-due bucket for prudential reporting purposes.

condition_context: {"loan_type": "all", "trigger": "non_performing_default", "conditions": "at least 90 calendar days elapsed since due date of contractual payment not met in full AND total amount unpaid equivalent to at least 90 days worth of contractual payments"}
consequence: Exposure is classified as non-performing (in default).

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "non_performing_non_restructured", "purpose": "reclassification_to_performing", "additional_conditions": "borrower has no exposure 90+ days past-due, borrower situation improved, exposure does not meet non-performing definition"}
consequence: ADI may reclassify the exposure from non-performing to performing once all criteria including this repayment period are met.

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "restructured", "purpose": "exit_from_restructured_classification", "additional_conditions": "all payments made in timely manner under revised contractual terms AND borrower has resolved financial difficulty"}
consequence: ADI may classify the restructured exposure as no longer restructured once the probation period and financial difficulty resolution criteria are met.

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "restructured_non_performing", "purpose": "reclassification_to_performing", "additional_conditions": "repayments made in timely manner per revised contractual terms, borrower has no exposure 90+ days past-due, borrower situation improved, exposure does not meet non-performing definition"}
consequence: ADI may reclassify a restructured non-performing exposure to performing only after the six-month probation period and all other criteria in paragraph 94 are met.
ADI must assume a marketing period of up to 12 months when determining the fair value of standard property security.

condition_context: {"property_type": "specialised_or_unusual", "purpose": "fair_value_of_security_determination", "condition": "professional valuers advise longer period is appropriate"}
consequence: ADI may adopt a marketing period of up to 24 months for specialised or unusual properties when professional valuers advise this is appropriate.

condition_context: {"applies_when": "ADI relies on a panel of approved valuation professionals"}
consequence: The panel of approved valuation professionals must be reviewed at least annually.
Minimum prescribed provision of 0% of balance outstanding applies for Category Two exposures up to 90 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 5% of balance outstanding applies for Category Two exposures 90 days and less than 182 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 10% of balance outstanding applies for Category Two exposures 182 days and less than 273 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 15% of balance outstanding applies for Category Two exposures 273 days and less than 365 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 20% of balance outstanding applies for Category Two exposures 365 days and over past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 0% of balance outstanding applies for Category Three exposures up to 90 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 40% of balance outstanding applies for Category Three exposures 90 days and less than 182 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 60% of balance outstanding applies for Category Three exposures 182 days and less than 273 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 80% of balance outstanding applies for Category Three exposures 273 days and less than 365 days past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 100% of balance outstanding applies for Category Three exposures 365 days and over past-due.
Minimum prescribed provision of 0% applies for Category Four exposures with irregularity period up to 14 days.
Minimum prescribed provision of 40% of balance outstanding applies for Category Four exposures with irregularity period 14 days and less than 90 days.
Minimum prescribed provision of 75% of balance outstanding applies for Category Four exposures with irregularity period 90 days and less than 182 days.
Minimum prescribed provision of 100% of balance outstanding applies for Category Four exposures with irregularity period 182 days and over.
Exposure qualifies as Category One (no prescribed provisions mandated) where LVR net of mortgage insurance does not exceed 80%.
Exposure is classified as Category Two and minimum prescribed provisions per Table 1 apply.

condition_context: {"category": "Category_Three", "loan_type": "residential_secured_mortgage", "note": "residential mortgage loans where LVR net of LMI exceeds 100% fall into Category Three"}
consequence: Residential mortgage exposures with LVR net of LMI exceeding 100% are classified as Category Three and minimum prescribed provisions per Table 2 apply.

Where the provision is adjusted to reflect fair value of residential property security, the minimum provision percentage is applied to the difference between the outstanding balance less LMI and 70% of the security value.
A reverse mortgage with LVR greater than 100% is treated as an indication of unlikeliness to pay and the exposure is classified as non-performing.
